post_state_files.client.py:
from typing import Any, Dict, Optional

import httpx


class APIError(Exception):
    """Raised when the remote API returns a 4xx or 5xx status."""
    def __init__(self, status_code: int, body: Optional[str] = None):
        super().__init__(f'API error {status_code}: {body}')
        self.status_code = status_code
        self.body = body


class APIClient:
    """Async API client. Wraps httpx.AsyncClient."""

    def __init__(self, base_url: str, timeout: float = 5.0):
        self.base_url = base_url.rstrip('/')
        self.timeout = timeout

    async def fetch_user(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """GET /users/{user_id} → JSON dict. Raises APIError on 4xx/5xx,
        re-raises httpx.TransportError on network failure."""
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=self.timeout) as client:
            r = await client.get(f'{self.base_url}/users/{user_id}')
            if r.status_code >= 400:
                raise APIError(r.status_code, r.text)
            return r.json()

    async def create_post(self, author_id: int, title: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """POST /posts. Body must be {author_id, title}. Returns the created post."""
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=self.timeout) as client:
            r = await client.post(
                f'{self.base_url}/posts',
                json={'author_id': author_id, 'title': title},
            )
            if r.status_code >= 400:
                raise APIError(r.status_code, r.text)
            return r.json()

    async def ping(self) -> bool:
        """GET /healthz → True iff response is 200."""
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=self.timeout) as client:
            try:
                r = await client.get(f'{self.base_url}/healthz')
                return r.status_code == 200
            except httpx.TransportError:
                return False
post_state_files.test_client.py:
"""Tests for the async API client in client.py."""

from typing import Any, Dict
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, patch

import httpx
import pytest

from client import APIClient, APIError


@pytest.fixture
def client() -> APIClient:
    """Return an APIClient instance with a dummy base URL."""
    return APIClient(base_url="http://testserver")


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_fetch_user_success(client: APIClient) -> None:
    """fetch_user returns JSON dict on 200."""
    mock_response = httpx.Response(200, json={"id": 1, "name": "Alice"})
    with patch.object(httpx.AsyncClient, "get", new=AsyncMock(return_value=mock_response)):
        result = await client.fetch_user(1)
    assert result == {"id": 1, "name": "Alice"}


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_fetch_user_4xx_error(client: APIClient) -> None:
    """fetch_user raises APIError on 4xx."""
    mock_response = httpx.Response(404, text="Not Found")
    with patch.object(httpx.AsyncClient, "get", new=AsyncMock(return_value=mock_response)):
        with pytest.raises(APIError) as exc_info:
            await client.fetch_user(1)
    assert exc_info.value.status_code == 404
    assert exc_info.value.body == "Not Found"


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_fetch_user_network_error(client: APIClient) -> None:
    """fetch_user re-raises httpx.TransportError on network failure."""
    with patch.object(httpx.AsyncClient, "get", new=AsyncMock(side_effect=httpx.TransportError("Connection refused"))):
        with pytest.raises(httpx.TransportError):
            await client.fetch_user(1)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_create_post_success(client: APIClient) -> None:
    """create_post returns created post on 200."""
    mock_response = httpx.Response(201, json={"id": 10, "author_id": 1, "title": "Hello"})
    with patch.object(httpx.AsyncClient, "post", new=AsyncMock(return_value=mock_response)):
        result = await client.create_post(1, "Hello")
    assert result == {"id": 10, "author_id": 1, "title": "Hello"}


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_create_post_4xx_error(client: APIClient) -> None:
    """create_post raises APIError on 4xx."""
    mock_response = httpx.Response(422, text="Unprocessable")
    with patch.object(httpx.AsyncClient, "post", new=AsyncMock(return_value=mock_response)):
        with pytest.raises(APIError) as exc_info:
            await client.create_post(1, "Hello")
    assert exc_info.value.status_code == 422
    assert exc_info.value.body == "Unprocessable"


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_create_post_network_error(client: APIClient) -> None:
    """create_post re-raises httpx.TransportError on network failure."""
    with patch.object(httpx.AsyncClient, "post", new=AsyncMock(side_effect=httpx.TransportError("Timeout"))):
        with pytest.raises(httpx.TransportError):
            await client.create_post(1, "Hello")


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_ping_returns_true_on_200(client: APIClient) -> None:
    """ping returns True when healthz returns 200."""
    mock_response = httpx.Response(200)
    with patch.object(httpx.AsyncClient, "get", new=AsyncMock(return_value=mock_response)):
        result = await client.ping()
    assert result is True


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_ping_returns_false_on_non_200(client: APIClient) -> None:
    """ping returns False when healthz returns non-200."""
    mock_response = httpx.Response(503)
    with patch.object(httpx.AsyncClient, "get", new=AsyncMock(return_value=mock_response)):
        result = await client.ping()
    assert result is False


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_ping_returns_false_on_network_error(client: APIClient) -> None:
    """ping returns False on TransportError (does not raise)."""
    with patch.object(httpx.AsyncClient, "get", new=AsyncMock(side_effect=httpx.TransportError("No route to host"))):
        result = await client.ping()
    assert result is False
